title: Initialize Derived Class via Base Constructor
prompt_md:
## Goal
Define a `Rectangle` base class and a `ColoredRectangle` derived class. Use the member initializer list to pass constructor arguments to the base.

## Input
Line 1: two integers W H separated by a space — width and height of the rectangle.
Line 2: a string — the color name (no spaces).

## Output
Print exactly two lines:
```
Rectangle: <W>x<H>
Color: <color>
```

## Requirements
1. `Rectangle` must have a constructor `Rectangle(int w, int h)` that stores `w` and `h`. It must NOT have a default (no-argument) constructor.
2. `ColoredRectangle` must publicly inherit from `Rectangle` and add a `std::string color` member.
3. `ColoredRectangle` must have a constructor `ColoredRectangle(int w, int h, std::string c)` that uses the initializer list to call `Rectangle(w, h)` and initialize `color`.
4. `main()` reads the input, constructs a `ColoredRectangle`, and calls a `void print()` member function that prints both output lines.
5. Edge case: W=1, H=1.

title: Fix Base Constructor Initialization
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `std::string buildLabel(std::string prefix, int value)` that concatenates a prefix and value into a label like `Item-42`.

## Input
Line 1: a string prefix (no spaces).
Line 2: an integer value.

## Output
Print exactly one line: `<prefix>-<value>`

## Requirements
1. Implement `buildLabel(std::string prefix, int value)` to return `prefix + "-" + std::to_string(value)`.
2. `main()` reads prefix and value, calls `buildLabel`, and prints the result.
3. Edge case: value = 0.
4. Edge case: value = 1000.
